sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to cleave, to divide, Arabic intrans. ثغر to be cleft, to open in fissures, ثَفْرُ aperture, chink, Eth. ሰዐረ፡ to dismiss, to set free (from the signification of opening, see Lud. de Dieu on Gen 23:10). Hence שַׁעַר No. 1, a gate.

(2) to estimate, to set a price (verbs of cleaving being often transferred to the sense of judging). Pro 23:7. Arab. سَعَرَ to set a price, سِعْرُ price of corn. Chald. שְׁעַר id. Hence שַׁעַר No. II. and pr.n. שְׁעַרְיָה.

II.
